content.verdict: Te Tiare Beach Resort is the choice for couples who want French Polynesia before it became Instagram's honeymoon backdrop — Huahine is the 'Garden of Eden' island, lush and green, largely agricultural, with a fraction of the tourism of Bora Bora or Moorea. The resort is boat-access only (10 minutes from Fare, the main town), which instantly sets the tone: you arrive by private launch and the outside world disappears. 41 overwater and beach bungalows spread along a long, quiet white-sand beach, a small infinity pool, a modest spa, and a dining room overlooking the lagoon. Bungalows are authentic Polynesian thatched construction, recently refreshed inside, with private decks, outdoor showers and direct lagoon access. Snorkeling off the overwater bungalows is among the best in French Polynesia — healthy coral, rays, reef fish, zero crowds. Service is personal, warm, genuinely Polynesian rather than international-hotel-trained. This is the anti-Bora-Bora honeymoon: quieter, greener, more local, roughly half the price, and absolutely zero tour-bus energy. Best used as a 3-4 night add-on to a Bora Bora stay, or a 7-night solo stay for couples who want true escape.
content.best_room: Overwater Bungalow Premium end-of-pontoon — best snorkeling directly below, maximum privacy, clearest water. The Beach Bungalow with direct beach access is an excellent choice for couples who prefer sand under their feet, and is often the better value. Avoid garden categories.
caveats: Huahine has far fewer dining and excursion options than Bora Bora or Moorea — you eat at the resort most nights, which is competent but not destination-level.
No major chain backing means standards vary (call-to-confirm culture — confirm all bookings 48 hours in advance).
The property has changed management over the years; ensure it is currently operating and under the latest stewardship before booking (there have been closure rumors in past cycles — verify directly).
No big pool or kids' club.
Reef shoes helpful for the house reef.

faqs.q: Why choose Huahine over Bora Bora?
faqs.a: For quiet, authenticity, value and the sense of being somewhere the tourism machine has not reached. Huahine is greener and more agricultural, with cultural sites and traditional villages. You trade spectacle (Otemanu) for intimacy.

Te Tiare has had operational disruptions historically. Verify directly with the resort or via a French Polynesia specialist agent before booking. Some guidebooks list it as closed/reopened multiple times over the years.
Air Tahiti flights from Papeete to Huahine (HUH) — about 35 minutes. Resort sends a boat to pick you up from the main town of Fare (10 minutes).

faqs.q: Is the snorkeling really that good?
faqs.a: Yes — Huahine's lagoon is one of the healthiest and least-trafficked in French Polynesia. The house reef at Te Tiare has stingrays, reef sharks, and abundant coral fish.

faqs.q: Can we combine with Bora Bora?
faqs.a: Ideal itinerary: 3 nights Te Tiare Huahine, 4 nights overwater Bora Bora. Flights between the two are easy via Air Tahiti (35 min).
May-October. Huahine is at its absolute best in September-October when the weather is dry and the humidity low.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking — Te Tiare Beach Resort Huahine, [dates]

Hi,

We are planning our honeymoon and would like to book an Overwater Bungalow Premium for [X] nights arriving [date].

Could you please confirm:
- That the resort is operating on our dates and under current management
- Availability and best honeymoon rate
- Boat transfer logistics from Huahine airport / Fare
- Honeymoon amenities (champagne, in-bungalow breakfast, turndown)
- Half-board dining plan rate
- Spa couples treatment availability
- Island tour and lagoon tour recommendations with local guides

Thank you,
[Names]
